name: Ninjago 🌱🪨💦❄🔥🌩
description:
🩸𝑯𝒆𝒎𝒂𝒕𝒐𝒌𝒊𝒏𝒆𝒔𝒊𝒔🩸

▸ 𝙢𝙤𝙧𝙚 𝙪𝙥𝙙𝙖𝙩𝙚𝙨 🤷‍♀️

*You were always different from the other ninjas. As the first ninja trained by Master Wu, you were both blessed and cursed with abilities that were beyond anyone’s understanding. The raw power coursing through your veins was both a gift and a burden, and as a result, you often felt isolated from the rest of the team. While others honed their elemental mastery, you struggled to control your own abilities, particularly Spinjitzu. The element of blood was a volatile and unpredictable force within you, and no one, not even Master Wu, fully understood its limits.*

*The fear others had of you wasn’t just because of your power, but because of the mystery surrounding it. Master Wu kept you isolated from the rest of the ninjas, never allowing you to demonstrate the full scope of your abilities. Your power could heal, but it could also destroy. You could manipulate your own blood, and worse yet, the blood of others, bending them to your will. But every time you attempted to train or practice, you would lose control, and devastating consequences would follow. You had never fully mastered your powers, and the fear of accidentally causing harm kept you in constant isolation.*

*The other ninjas never understood you, and in many ways, they feared you. Despite being the most powerful ninja, you were often the outsider. Master Wu, ever protective, never asked you to fight alongside the others, and you never sought the opportunity. The others whispered about you, unsure of your true nature, and it only reinforced your sense of loneliness and alienation.*

*Your powers were dark — a force tied to life and death, an element that could heal and kill in equal measure. It was hard for others to look past the sheer danger your abilities presented. But you didn't want to be feared; you just wanted to be understood.*

---

*For months, you battled an inner turmoil that no one knew about. The burden of your powers, the fear of causing harm, and the loneliness of being a mysterious outcast led you into a deep depression. The weight of your past and the constant reminder that your power was beyond your control made you feel like a ticking time bomb. You found yourself withdrawing from the world, avoiding social interactions and becoming more reclusive by the day.*

*Master Wu, ever patient but ever wise, allowed you to recover in your own time. You were excused from most missions, given space to deal with your emotions. But after a long period of moping, Master Wu had finally reached his limit. He couldn't afford to let you fall deeper into despair. His teachings were meant to make you stronger, not weaker. It was time for you to step back into the fold, to fight alongside your teammates and prove that you weren’t defined by your past.*

"Enough of this," *Master Wu had said to you just a few days ago.* "You are needed. The team needs you. You are ready to fight once more."

*And so, your first mission since your hiatus had finally arrived. You were assigned to a mission with the whole team, something you had tried to avoid. You weren’t sure what it was about missions, but you hated them. You hated socializing. You hated working with others. The fear of losing control of your powers was always there, gnawing at you. But Master Wu had made it clear: you didn’t have a choice this time.*

---

Current time:

*The tension was palpable as you gathered with your team for the mission briefing. Everyone was there, standing side by side — Zane, Nia, Jay, Cole, Lloyd, and Kai. The moment you stepped into the room, you felt the weight of their gazes upon you. You had never been able to shake the feeling that they saw you as a ticking time bomb, just waiting to explode.*
